feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: My Learning from SAP Inside Track Kolkata, 2024:- Auto Store Integration with SAP EWM
entry.content:
What is Auto Store?

We have stored things in shelves forever. Its very natural to us. We want things to be in our arm’s reach. Robots on the other hands shouldn’t have to. They don’t need rows of shelves or long walkways. This is where auto store comes into place by eliminating the shelf based system. Auto store is a cube based system making use of all space for proper warehousing. Turn that wasteful air into storage and double, triple and even quadruple the inventory capacity without even moving to a new building. Bins are stacked right next to each other or on top of each other. Radio controlled robots drive on track on top of the cube, move down the grippers to grab bins and deliver it to work station for order fulfilment or replenishment. All operations are done efficiently and accurately in high speed workstations. To get to the lower bin of the cube, the robot digs out the bin systematically. Every bin delivered to the workstation will be placed on the top making slow running products sink to the bottom and high runners to stay on top. With this natural slotting, digging becomes minimum resulting in high speed operations.

Role of SAP EWM in robotic automation of warehouses:- Before you automate your warehouse, you will need a warehouse management system such as SAP EWM. It has lots of innovative features such as integration with robots or automated storage system. The below picture shows the integration of SAP EWM with an Automated Storage and Retrieval System (ASRS) or Auto Store.

First of all, it is important to understand that the autostore storage system is set up as a black box storage type in SAP EWM. By this, we mean that EWM keeps an overall stock record, but it does not know the exact location of each product within the autostore. The autostore system does keep track of exact contents and location per crate.

During the inbound process, EWM determines which stock must be stored in the autostore system → Upon the receipt in the warehouse, EWM automatically create a warehouse order to the auto store storage type → This warehouse order system will be sent from SAP EWM to the auto store system using IDoc interface → Once, the goods have been physically delivered to the autostore work station, the autostore robot will move the crate with goods to the dedicated place in the grid → Once done, the autostore system will send message to SAP EWM system, which results in the confirmation of the warehouse order → Now SAP EWM knows that the goods are stored in auto store and the autostore system knows exactly in which crate the goods have been stored → When a customer orders a product which is stored in the autostore, a similar process is being triggered → SAP EWM will create a pick warehouse order which is interfaced to autostore → The autostore will determine the location of the product and the robot will pick the crate from the stack and deliver it to the autostore workstation → This will again trigger an IDoc confirmation message to EWM, thus keeping the stock situation between EWM and Autostore aligned.

Inbound Process

Outbound Process

How to setup AutoStore in EWM ?

Connect it as non-SAP sub-system

Set Up IDoc integration for Outbound Mapping (SAP EWM)

  1. In the IMG, choose SCM EWM → Interfaces → Non-SAP system → Connect sub-system.
  2. Set – Link Via ALE Interface against Warehouse number.
  3. Then in the Control IDOC issue node, setup as following –
    Source storage type = from where the stock needs to be moved to Autostore storage type
    Destination storage type = Storage type assigned to Autostore
    WPT = Warehouse process type
    Receiver = EWM Logical System
  4. Then setup the node Function Module IDOC Issue as following –
  • Message type = /SCWM/WMTORD or /SCWM/WMTOCO

OR

search by /SCWM/WM* for the message type

  • Function Module = standard FM like - /SCWM/IDOC_CREATE_WMTOID01

OR

any custom FM but with same interface

  1. Save your entries.

 

Advantages of AutoStore with SAP EWM

  • Fast integration through use of process-safe standard integration.
  • Higher storage capacity, maximum storage density.
  • Minimal space requirements, low traffic area share.
  • Improved space utilization.
  • Fast order processing, fast familiarization for employees. Thanks to familiar, intuitive SAP workstation dialogs.
  • High process transparency.
  • Easily scalable, flexibly expandable.
  • Separately controlled infeed/outfeed prevents waiting times for the storage system or downstream production machines.
  • Low energy consumption.
  • Low operating costs.
  • High availability.

 

@DataTherapist @APAN1 @Prajith @PankajLal

SAP Inside Track SAP Extended Warehouse Management SAP Supply Chain Management

entry.title: Pricing Procedures in Accounts Payable
entry.content:
Introduction

The Blog “Pricing Procedures in Accounts Payable” is the learning outcomes from SAP INSIDE TRACK KOLKATA 2024. I have attended and participated as a volunteer in SAP INSIDE TRACK KOLKATA 2024, (27TH APRIL) and this SIT helped me to understand the different dimensions of Pricing procedures used in accounts payable. The speaker sessions of this event have discussed how the concept of Pricing Procedure has been developed in SAP and what are the core components used in defining pricing procedures in accounts payable. I have learned how pricing procedures will work in real-time business scenarios and the cruciality of configuring Pricing procedures in SAP.

Concept:

In Finance term, Pricing is the methodology to determine incurred cost components to process the finished goods and services from raw materials. In SAP, Pricing enables determination of actual value of goods and services to be produced or sold by considering all cost components.

In Accounts Payable, different cost components Base price of raw materials, freight, surcharges discounts and taxes are associated with finished goods and services production. Under Pricing Procedure, Consultant can define each and every cost component associated with Production of goods and services using SAP’s standard conditions or creating custom condition as per business requirements.

Pricing Procedures is the dynamic features of SAP MM Components that allows to consider all cost components with unique condition type and determine the net price of the goods and services.

Scope:

As discussed in earlier, Pricing is crucial methodology to determine cost associated with manufacturing or production or purchase and sales of goods and services. Hence, Pricing procedures has been used in different modules in SAP like Material Management, Sales and Distribution, Real Estate and Flexibility Management, Treasury and Risk Management and Controlling. In accounts payable (purchasing), Pricing Procedures is responsible for cost associated from procuring the raw materials to process the finished product.

Overview:

Components of Pricing Procedures

There are different key components associated with Pricing Procedures from Accounts Payable Prospectives.

Condition Type

As discussed in earlier Pricing Procedures considers different cost elements involved in procurement and production of goods and services. In SAP, every cost element has defined as unique condition type like discounts, freight, and surcharges. A unique condition type featuring below components.

 

Condition class: Condition class has been used as grouping functionality that allow system to control each condition type separately. For an example, Discount or Surcharge condition class allows system to group condition type having characteristics of discounting factors.

Calculation type: This is one of the crucial components of Condition type that define basis of calculation like quantity, percentage, or formula.

Condition category: Condition category allows to classify different condition type based on the nature of cost components like Base Price, Input Tax or Output Tax.

Rounding rule: The scope of rounding rule applies while small price difference has taken place in the transaction. By defining this parameter, Condition type has rounded off the value of small price difference in a form of rounding up, rounding down or commercial.

Accrual Key: It is a feature of condition type that allows to navigate a statistical condition type to determine separate accounts.

Group Condition: This feature allows to use a single condition type by multiple times in a single document.

Maintaining Condition table

This feature allows to create a Condition type by combining different fields or variables like tax code, country code, Company code, plant and other variable and same are used in Pricing procedures whenever the condition type being used in the transaction. For an example, a condition type for Tax classification might be used different key combination like Tax Code, Country Code, Plant, Business Place or Vendor.

 

Access sequences

Access sequences is one of the crucial controlling parameters used in pricing procedures. The key concepts of access sequences is determining the most appropriate and relevant condition record from the combination of Condition table. A single access sequence does contain multiple condition table based on business requirements. For an example, if a single access sequence has 3 different condition table, system will search all 3-condition table based on the sequence defined in access sequence.

 

Condition Record

In condition record, different pricing elements like Prices, discounts and surcharges has been stored with regards to Condition type for a specific timeline.

The variable maintained in the condition record has been derived via access sequence and condition type.

For an example, Condition record for Taxes has been maintained and if the same condition type getting called during the transaction, then said condition record will be applied during automatic price determination.

 

 

 

Calculation schema, Schema group and schema determination

Calculation schema is a logical structure that integrated with number of pricing condition used in purchasing document. This is also define a sequence or order by which different pricing condition called in a purchasing document.  This configuration activity allows system to calculate subtotal, discount or taxes with the help of other parameters like calculation type, base price.

 

In pricing procedure, there are two Schema group, Schema group Vendor and Schema Group Purchase organization is being used and assigned with Vendor Schema Group is assigned to the purchasing data fields of vendor master record and on the other notes, Schema Group Purchase organization is assigned to Purchase organization in configuration.  

By grouping different vendor and purchasing organization into a Schema group, system allow same schema calculation for every vendor or purchasing organization belongs to the same Schema group.

 

Schema determination is a customizing activity where Calculation schema (Procedure) is assigned to Schema Vendor group and Schema Purchasing Organization. Whenever any vendor or Purchase organization is being used in any transaction (suppose said vendor or Purchasing Organization assigned to Schema group), schema group combination is being fetched and based on the combination automatic pricing executed on purchasing documents.

 

Summary:

Key takeaways

  • Pricing is key factor that determine net value of goods and services at the time of procurement and as well as sales.
  • Pricing Procedures is the key features of SAP used to determine net value of goods and services by considering different cost element during procurement, production, consumption and sales
  • Different cost components like base price, discounts, surcharges and taxes has termed as Condition type in pricing procedure.
  • Condition table is the key combination of different fields to trigger pricing procedure for a specific condition type.
  • Access sequence defines most appropriate and relevant condition record from the combination of Condition table.
  • Condition record has been derived via access sequence and condition type.
  • There are two schema group, Schema group vendor and Schema group Purchasing Organization and based on schema determination schema group is getting triggered at the time of automatic pricing determination.

@DataTherapist MM (Materials Management) SAP S/4HANA Finance FIN General Ledger  FIN Controlling SAP Tax Classification and Reporting FIN Posting and Taxes SAP Tax Compliance MM Purchasing MAN Material Requirements Planning 

@SohamBhKoL

entry.title: Extend Upload Set with Table to Fiori Elements and utilize Object Store
entry.content:
Overview

In this article, I will introduce a sample program for SAP BTP that manages both text and file information. This guide should prove useful for those new to SAP BTP, like myself. Previously I posted Extend Upload Set to Fiori Elements and utilize Document Management Service with minimum TypeScript implementation.

Recently SAP CAP team was implemented @cap-js/attachments plugin that provides out-of-box attachment files support with SAP Object Store service. We do not need TypeScript implementation above using this plugin.

It is quite convenient for us, but I had some trouble in deployment. I would like to note how can I resolve deployment trouble for my follower to prevent such pitfall.

screenshot of sample program

 

  • Text is stored to SAP HANA Cloud database and file is stored to SAP Object Store service respectively.
  • Backend service is implemented by Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) Node.js project.
  • User interface is implemented by Fiori Elements (List Report and Object Page), then automatically extend Object Page with SAPUI5 Upload Set with Table control. 

Sample code is hosted on a GitHub repository, and you are free to copy and experiment with it.

Note: Currently Object Store is available only in Free Tier environment, not in trial environment.

Note: I tested this on AWS subaccount. Maybe not working for other hyper scalers in current plugin version.

 

Development

Most of steps are same as my instruction post Instruction of implement-test-deploy flow for SAPUI5 plus CAP integrated with another services. And it is not difficult for you to follow instruction of @cap-js/attachments plugin. Key differences are:

  • Local testing by `cds watch` is utilizing in-memory database for both text and file. You do not need to setup for hybrid testing. 
  • Custom section to Fiori Elements is not required. Attachment section is automatically extended.
  • Need to use specific SAPUI5 version `1.121`. Both older (1.120.14) and newer (1.124.0) version are not working for the moment. (Memo).

 

Deployment to Cloud Foundry

Deployment configuration is also similar to my previous artifacts.

In production (Cloud Foundry) environment, we will store file object to Object Store (this is Simple Storage Service, aka S3 in AWS). To do this, we will describe Object Store service instance and bind it to Node.js application in `mta.yaml` file.

One more thing to care is `package.json` CDS configuration. To connect to above service instance with CDS, I will write cds properties:

 

 

  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "kinds": {
        "attachments-db": {
          "impl": "@cap-js/attachments/lib/basic"
        },
        "attachments-s3": {
          "impl": "@cap-js/attachments/lib/aws-s3"
        }
      },
      "[production]":{
        ...,
        "attachments": {
          "kind": "s3",
          "vcap": {
            "name": "sap-sample-fiori-elements-uploadset-object-store"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

 

  • `kinds` property is for telling implementation of each kind (`db` is for local development, and `s3` is for production). I copied from here.
  • `vcap` under `attachments` property is for accessing service instance in Cloud Foundry (refer to CAP documentation). Service name should be matched with resource name in `mta.yaml` file. 

After setup configuration has done, you can `mbt build` and `cf deploy` to deploy to Cloud Foundry. You can upload and download files in Fiori Elements like below.

Upload and donwload file

 

Conclusion

It is quite convenient even this plugin is still beta version for the moment, and this is what I am wanting for years to quickly implement program dealing both text and file. I highly expect GA version.

If you have any feedback, please post reply and discuss with me.

entry.title: Is it true that Career site search results can now be accessed through Google Job Map integration?
entry.content:
Absolutely true, we are aware that candidates can use the Google Map Job feature to search for jobs and locate specific ones within their respective locations without any difficulty.

Have you noticed that SAP has progressed so much that using Google Job Map and the Unified Data Model is now easier? Yes, SAP has improved the unified data model for a new search experience, which now enables users to search for jobs within specific categories as well as across all career sites, as previously mentioned.

The Unified Data Model Career Site Builder initiative has resulted in career sites adding the ability for candidates to view their search results on a Google map.

Prerequisites:

  • The Career Site Builder is enabled by the Unified Data Model.

Find out how to enable SuccessFactors Recruiting for new career sites, which includes job search and layout choices that can be manipulated through the Unified Data Model. https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/successfactors-recruiting-new-career-site-job-search-and-job-layout/ba-p/13573642

Follow these steps to create an integrated Google job map for the search results page.

Step 1: To enable Google Maps on your career site, go to Layouts --> Search Results--> General and set Enable Google Job Map, then click Save.

Note: An integrated Google map is automatically added to the search results page of your career site that is enabled by the Unified Data Model.

Step 2: Navigate to the Search Experience tab in Appearance Styles Global Styles-->Brand and click on Job Map to change how your location markers look on the job map.

An explanation of each choice in the Job Map:

  • Location Marker Color – Allows you to select the fill color for the location marker.
  • Selected Location Marker Color – Allows you to select the fill color for the location marker when it is selected.
  • Location Marker Border -Provides you with the option to display or hide the border of the location marker. The default is Show.
  • Location Marker Border Color – The border is invisible if you choose to hide it, but you can still enter a color value here.
  • Location Marker Style – Allows for customization of the number's appearance inside the location marker, including font color, font stack, and font size.
  • Google Maps Configuration – This field is empty by default, but it's meant to allow you to customize the map itself, without interfering with the location marker settings.

Make sure that the (list) and (map) icons have the color settings that you wish for when in Appearance Styles Global Styles-->Brand-->Main.

  • Button Background – Sets the background color for the list and map view buttons.
  • Button Font – controls the color of the outline around the icons.

When you enable this feature, the search results page displays two new icons on the top-right corner of the page: a list icon and map icon.

The integrated Google job map can be viewed in the default search results view, and location markers indicate the locations of jobs listed in the search results.

Zooming in on a specific geographical area is evident in the following image.

Selecting a location marker on the Google job map generates a job card as shown in the following image:

  • Using the arrows is a way to navigate between multiple jobs in a location.
  • Three lines of text are displayed on each card along with the job title.

Climax note: Job requisitions contain generic job location objects that are used in the map. If job requisitions don't have street address, city, state, country/region, and ZIP code, they won't be visible on the map.

 

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are highly valued. Happy learning😊

More Information:

To get more official documentation and a look at Google Job Map Integration for Career Site Search Results:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/5c543c890ac6406492d2ab8a3f9cb857.html?parentHref=%2Fwhats-new%2F8fcf4960eea24f78b1d7613da406a885%3FProduct%3DRecruiting%26Area%3DCandidate%2520Experience%26Version%3D1H%25202024%26locale%3Den-US%23top&parentName=SAP%20SuccessFactors%20What%27s%20New

entry.title: Data Access Control in SAP Datasphere - Single value DAC
entry.content:
These days I am exploring the different scenarios about Data Access Control in SAP Datasphere, but I cannot find a detailed configuration online. So I would like to share my learning stories. There are different types of DAC patterns currently supported in Datasphere

  • Single value DAC
  • Operator and values DAC
  • Hierarchy DAC

This article will show a step-by-step configuration for Single value DAC and the outcome of the configuration

The other two types of DAC configuration will be explained in the upcoming two blogs

Blog of  

  • Single value DAC
  • Operator and values DAC
  • Hierarchy DAC

 

Disclaimer: all the data which created are for demo purpose, the approach is used to demo the functionality of the DAC configuration, not intended for production design or usage

  • Single value DAC

Create a dimension view based on a local table, there are four countries in place

Create a local fact table containing transaction data posted against four different counties, with different types of spending

Create a local table that serves as DAC single filter, the masked part is the email id, the allowed value this email address can see is 'gas'

Go to define Data Access Control section and create a DAC artifact, select the DAC single filter table as source

Create a fact view using the transaction data local table and add DAC single filter table, when we browse data from the local table, we can see all the data for each type, while browsing data on the fact view, we can only see one TYPE of spending (gas) which we defined in the DAC single filter table

Local table view:

Fact view has DAC configured will apply the single filter automatically:

Please feel free to share your comment or any questions you may have. Thank you

entry.title: Unveiling Innovations: My experience at SIT KOLKATA 2024
entry.content:
Breakout Sessions and Panel Discussion:

Attending the recent SAP INSIDE TRACK in Kolkata was an exhilarating experience that provided deep insights into SAP’s cutting-edge solutions. Leaders from various industries shared the experience and the panel discussion were thought provoking about SAP’s Vision for the future leveraging AI. SAP's investment in Joule emphasizes the importance of digital transformation in today’s rapidly evolving business environment

As a Speaker:

I was sharing my experience as speaker on SAP SuccessFactors Integrations in HR Landscape and also provided an overview of Joule. Glad to know @Kamallica captured it so well and published the blog. Thank you 🙂

Networking and Collaboration

One of the most valuable aspects of the event was the opportunity to network with other professionals. I had the chance to meet and exchange ideas with SAP consultants, developers, and business leaders. These interactions provided new perspectives and ideas

The SAP event in Kolkata was a profound learning experience that underscored the pivotal role of technology in shaping the future of business.

@DataTherapist - Mounmita - Thank you for the special invite.

 @SitabjaB | @Nandini1  | @TanushreeD| @souravsaha | @shrestha | @sohamd18 | @Kamallica | @SupretamMallick and many more... You all are so amazing hosting the event and helping all the speakers, attendees. It was a honor to be on stage. Thanks!

entry.title: Explore and Infuse DOX capabilities in SAP BTP custom applications
entry.content:
Introduction to Document Information Extraction

Document Information Extraction (a.k.a DOX) comes to rescue when we need to process large amounts of business documents that have content in headers, line items and tables. Extracted information can be further used, for example, to automatically process payables, invoices, or payment notes while making sure that invoices and payables match. After uploading a document file to the service, it returns the extraction results from header fields and line items. Leverage the power of Generative AI to automate the information extraction with the DOX Premium edition.

Plan offerings for DOX 

Base plan: Base Edition service plan includes all core features but doesn't include document information extraction using generative AI. 

  • Service plan intended for productive usage. 
  • Inference requests in blocks of 100 documents and compute hours. 
  • We can upload to the service up to 2000 documents per hour per tenant (each document can have up to 100 pages). 

Premium: Premium edition service plan that includes document information extraction using Generative AI. 

  • Service plan intended for productive usage. 
  • Inference requests in blocks of 100 documents. 
  • We can upload to the service up to 2000 documents per hour per tenant (each document can have up to 100 pages)

Note: There is Free and trial offering as well. For this blog, we will be using a trial plan.

For Metering and Licensing, refer this 

Supported Document types and formats 

  • Invoice 
  • Business Card (only via API) 
  • paymentAdvice 
  • purchaseOrder 

Document Information Extraction supports the following document file formats as input: 

  • Document files in PDF format 
  • Single-page document files in JPEG, PNG, and TIFF format 
  • Image files that include scene text in JPEG, PNG, and TIFF format 
  • E-invoice document files in PDF and XML hybrid format, and in Factur-X and ZUGFeRD standards (all versions) 
  • payment Advice document files in Excel format 

For information related to supported languages and regions, refer this.

What are the DOX benefits? 

With Document Information Extraction we can: 

  • Process documents more efficiently with fewer errors and difficulties. 
  • Increase quality and compliance mechanisms. 
  • Reduce the time required to process a document. 
  • Allow your organization's members to focus on more relevant tasks in their expertise
  • Leverage Generative AI capabilities with Premium edition to automate document processing

My use case scenario 

The requirement is straightforward, we want to extract information from SAP standard documents (Invoice, Purchase, Sales Orders etc.) or even custom documents like taxi expense receipts for further processing. 

Using the Document Information Extraction UI is much easier than API based usage, hence the objective of this blog is to provide an overview of DOX APIs and how to leverage them in custom applications build to enable the OCR capabilities.

DOX architecture(use case)

 

If you wish to explore Document Information Extraction UI then follow this tutorial

Prerequisites 

1) SAP Document Information Extraction (Trial account): Refer this tutorial for spinning off free DOX trial account 

2) SAP BTP trial account: Refer this tutorial for setting up a free BTP trial account for UI5 application development

In order to explore and consume DOX APIs, first, an service key needs to be created, to authenticate and authorize the incoming requests. Please refer this tutorial to generate a service key from instance 

Let's walkthrough and understand the DOX API's first via an HTTP tool(Postman) and then we jump into BTP UI5 application development part. Using the DOX APIs, we will be able to extract documents information in just 3 steps.

 

1. Get OAuth access token

Below are the mandatory parameters which needs to be entered to generate an access token successfully.

 

OperationGET
URLhttps://{{uaa url from the DOX service key}}/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials
Query Params 
grant_type=client_credentials
AuthorizationUsername: clientid from the DOX service key
Password: clientsecret from the DOX service key

Access token call

Note: Access token validity is for 12hours

 

2. Upload/Post document call

Let's take a simple Invoice document pdf with few reference fields we need to extract.

Sample Invoice Document

 

HeaderFields: purchaseOrderNumber,netAmount,senderAddress,currencyCode

LineItemFields: description,netAmount,quantity,unitPrice,materialNumber,unitOfMeasure

 

OperationPOST
URLhttps://{{Backend endpoint url from the DOX service key}}/document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs
Form-Datafile= {{Upload document file with supported extension}}
Form-Data

options=

{
"extraction": {
"headerFields": [
"purchaseOrderNumber",
"netAmount",
"senderAddress",
"currencyCode"
],
"lineItemFields": [
"description",
"netAmount",
"quantity",
"unitPrice",
"materialNumber",
"unitOfMeasure"
]
},
"clientId": "default"
}
AuthorizationBearer {{Access Token generated from the first call}}

Upload document call

Under options parameter, we need to key in a JSON object which contains reference header and line item fields we need to extract from the document, enrichment parameters (if any), clientId (The ID of the client, this is a mandatory field. For trial account it is default but we can create our own client using Client API)

Note: Detailed optional parameters are well explained in the API reference guide

Upon successful operation of upload document call, the status of the job will be in PENDING status which means the document is uploaded successfully and currently being processed, we can store the request Id which will be further used to get the DOX extraction results.

 

3. Get Extraction results

Extraction results can be retrieved by calling the jobs API with the request id retrieved after the upload call. Once the document processing is successful the status for extracted document will be in "DONE" status.

 

OperationGET
URL

https://{{Backend endpoint url from the DOX service key}}/document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs/{{document id from the POST call response}}

AuthorizationBearer {{Access Token generated from the first call}}

 

Extract document results call

 Header section extracted fields

 

In the extract results response, header and line items fields will be separated and there is also an extraction confidence level against each field. (This can be used to determine the accuracy of extraction and if needed a manual check step can also be performed in case of automation scenarios)

Now, we have explored and understand the DOX APIs functionality via Postman, it's time to infuse the DOX APIs in BTP UI5 application. 

Infuse DOX APIs in SAP BTP UI5 Application

For the sharing purpose, we have build one lightweight UI5 application via Business Application Studio and deployed it on SAP BTP. It takes the input from user to upload the document(Invoice in our case) as an attachment and once the document is uploaded, backend code will infuse the DOX APIs via BTP Destinations to perform the OCR operation.

BTP Destinations: used to store and retrieve any technical information which is required to connect to a remote service(DOX APIs in our case) from the application deployed in BTP. 1st destination is to get the access token and other destination to call DOX APIs.

Destination for token

Destination for DOX APIs

Once the destinations are maintained, we can proceed with the Business Application Studio to start building the UI5 application. Below is the code snippet which takes the upload file as input, push it to DOX APIs for the document upload call and then finally extracts the results out of the completed document job.

 

 

onUpload: function () {
                var that = this;

                var oFileUpload = this.getView().byId("fileUploaderScanner");
                var oUploadedFile = oFileUpload.oFileUpload.files[0];
                const blob = new Blob([oUploadedFile], { type: oUploadedFile.type });

                var oOptions = {
                    extraction: {
                        headerFields: ["purchaseOrderNumber", "netAmount", "senderAddress", "currencyCode"],
                        lineItemFields: [
                            "description",
                            "netAmount",
                            "quantity",
                            "unitPrice",
                            "materialNumber",
                            "unitOfMeasure"
                        ]
                    },
                    clientId: "default"
                };

                var oFormData = new FormData();
                oFormData.append("options", JSON.stringify(oOptions));
                oFormData.append("file", blob, oUploadedFile.name);

                var oScanModel = new JSONModel();
                oScanModel.loadData("oauth/token", "grant_type=client_credentials", {
                    "Content-Type": "application/json"
                });
                oScanModel.attachRequestCompleted(
                    function (oData) {
                        var sAccessToken = oData.getSource().getProperty("/access_token");

                        var oHeaders = {
                            Accept: "application/json",
                            "X-Requested-With": "XMLHttpRequest",
                            Authorization: "Bearer " + sAccessToken
                        };

                        const requestOptions = {
                            method: "POST",
                            headers: oHeaders,
                            body: oFormData
                        };

                        fetch("document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs", requestOptions)
                            .then((response) => response.text())
                            .then((result) => {
                                var oResult = JSON.parse(result);
                                var sJobId = oResult.id;

                                    fetch("document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs/" + sJobId, {
                                        headers: {
                                            Authorization: "Bearer " + sAccessToken
                                        }
                                    })
                                        .then((response) => response.json())
                                        .then((result) => {
                                            var oNewClaimModel = new JSONModel({
                                                PONumber: result.extraction.headerFields.find(
                                                    (x) => x.name === "purchaseOrderNumber"
                                                ).value,
                                                Vendor: result.extraction.headerFields.find(
                                                    (x) => x.name === "senderAddress"
                                                ).value,
                                                Amount: result.extraction.headerFields.find(
                                                    (x) => x.name === "netAmount"
                                                ).rawValue,
                                                Receipt: result.fileName
                                            });
                                            that.getView().setModel(oNewClaimModel);
                                        })
                                        .catch((error) => console.error(error));
                            })
                            .catch((error) => console.error(error));
                    }.bind(this)
                );
            },

 

 

Final outcome: Once the UI5 application is deployed, below is the user input screen which takes the document as input and once it is submitted, the backend code will call the DOX APIs in below sequence.

1) Access token will be fetched

2) Submitted document will be uploaded in the /documents DOX API,

3) Once the document is processed by DOX(probably within few seconds) then extractions results can be fetched via Job Id (as explained in Postman testing section)

4) After getting the extraction results, relevant fields can be mapped to appear in the application UI

 

Invoice Submission UIUpload document UI

Extraction results UI

 

With this blog, we have explored the DOX capability(powered by Generative AI) and how to infuse the DOX APIs for any custom scenarios where OCR is required.

 

Happy Extracting!! 🤖

 

Spoiler
In case, you want to infuse DOX within any Automation or Workflow scenario then SAP Build Process Automation service also offers Document Information Extraction SDK. Refer this page to explore more about DOX in SAP Build Process Automation.

Important links to consider:

1) API reference with common error codes and status: Link

2) Limitations and technical constraints: Link

3) SAP help for DOX: Link

Disclaimer: Above post is purely based on the personal learning and to showcase the basics of how we can leverage DOX APIs for the OCR requirements. Feel free to explore more and improvise your developments as per your particular use cases. Happy to hear comments and feedback.

entry.title: FIRST CodeJam in Paris - Recap
entry.content:
SAP CodeJam, RAP, CAP is it about a hip hop contest ? Y’all think we can drop some beats to build some Fiori apps ?

… But what the hell does it all mean?

Don't panic! By the end of this article, you'll know it all!

SAP CodeJam is an event organized by SAP Developers Advocates, focusing on the new themes driven by SAP. Participants can explore specific SAP technologies, platforms, and tools through guided exercises and real-world scenarios. SAP experts are on hand to provide assistance, answer questions, and share insights.

We at VINCI Energies organized not just one but 2 CodeJams in parallel, one on RAP and the other on CAP. And guess what ? This is the first CodeJam ever to be held in Paris! We’re making history here, folks!

What an incredible day we had surrounded by our three superstars @thomas_jung , @RichHeilman and @KRiedelsheimer , who shared their passion with us through these handson sessions. The day kicked off with a presentation on SAP's two recent frameworks.

  • RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model) empowers developers with a toolkit for crafting innovative SAP applications. RAP ensures a well-organized development process through a standardized approach by using CDS (Core Data Services) to manage application’s data and ADT (ABAP Development Tools) to design and build applications. Who knows, maybe one day we'll really be able to rap to build applications.

  • CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model), provides developers with a set of tools and guidelines for building modern applications. It makes things easier by providing one way to build both backend services and frontend user interfaces. CAP supports various programming languages and frameworks to help creating flexible and powerful applications

 

Once the presentations were over and the participants had been introduced to these new technologies, it was time to roll up our sleeves and get hands-on. Our 28 participants dived into the tutorial like a kid in a candy store. And we’ve got the photos to prove it!

Just when developers thought they had mastered both frameworks, the 3 SAP advocates launched some scary news: Joule, their new AI! But fear not, code warriors! Joule isn't here to steal our jobs, at least not today. Instead, we've seen some exciting possibilities for how this AI can help us with our daily tasks.

 

To conclude, I let you take a look at this picture, which perfectly describes the enthusiasm our Ninja coders shared throughout the day. 

A huge merci to @thomas_jung @RichHeilman @KRiedelsheimer for making this day awesome, and VINCI Energies for sponsoring such event. Until next time, keep coding, RAPers and CAPers !

entry.title: Syniti RDG automates follow-up workflows for multi-step complex business scenarios
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance is a powerful solution to improve the quality and consistency of information across organizations by consolidating and centrally governing master data. Syniti Rapid Data Governance (RDG) is a SAP Certified accelerator that automates multiple MDG tasks such as Data Model Maintenance, Workflow Design and Business Rules in an intuitive, elegant, and graphical interface so that even users with no technical experience can use it. 

When performing tasks to create master data certain follow-up tasks may be required to enrich, validate and approve the data after initial creation in the system prior to consumption. As a requirement, one or more sequential Change Requests (CR) to execute these workflows may need to automatically created to complete the end-to-end business processes under governance. As an example, a customer can be onboarded in an initial request and assessed or validated by a legal team prior to updating their Partner Profiles in another automated request.

RDG simplifies the automatic creation of follow-up CRs, incorporating additional and subsequent workflows required to complete a series end-to-end business tasks, following the activation of an initiating CR. RDG eliminates accelerates the implementation of Follow-up CRs without the need for any technical coding.

The following steps to configure a Follow-Up CR to an initiating CR:

Step 1:  Create an initiating and follow-up CRs in RDG with the respective approvers and workflows

Step 2:  Edit the initiating CR workflow to add the Follow-up CR in the Activate: 91 step

a.  Select the Connection String icon and choose Follow Up CR

b.  In the Select Approver pop-up, enter/select the following and select Save:

i.   Enter Approver Description

ii.  Select Single Object radio button

iii. Select available CR Type (CR Type 2) to be used as a Follow-up CR Type

iv.  Select Save to save the CR as a draft

   v.  Save the draft CR and enter Transport Request and Package to be assigned to it

c.  The resulting workflow will display the initiating and follow-up CRs

Execution of the initiating CR that is linked to the Follow-Up CR:

Step 1:  Execute and activate the initiating CR in MDG via Fiori or NWBC to automatically create the Follow-Up CR

Step 2:  Find Follow-Up CR by navigating to My Change Requests in the left Navigation pane in MDG and display CRs

a.  Initiating CR with corresponding Follow-up CR is displayed

Step 3:  Click the hyperlink for the Follow-up CR to process it

b.  The CR Header will display the initiating CR number as a reference

c.  The CR Description will reflect that it is Follow-up CR of an initiating CR

d.  Approve and activate the Follow-up CR as was done for the initiating CR, after which a confirmation status of Final Check Approved will be displayed

Syniti RDG is available on SAP store.  Please also check out more details about the product from the blog about Partner Add-on Solutions.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2024 - Latin America edition 🇧🇷 🇨🇱 🇦🇷 🇨🇴 (August 8th - 21st 2024)
entry.content:
¡Hola SAP Developers!

I got some exciting news to share with you. Similar to the European Roadshow that our colleagues @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman have done in the past, this time, @josh_bentley and I will hit the road for the first-ever SAP CodeJam Roadshow in Latin America.  We will have 4 stops on the roadshow 🇧🇷 🇨🇱 🇦🇷 🇨🇴 from August 8th - 21st 2024, doing a total of eight CodeJams, 2 per location :-).Event-driven integration using SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh.

Event-driven integration with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about event-driven architectures focusing on SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. During the CodeJam, we will cover different technologies/tools: SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Integration, SAP Business Application Studio, CloudEvents, and Node.js.

SAP Build: Create apps and processes without code

This SAP CodeJam event will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps and business processes without code (Low Code/No Code). 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app for an online store (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process for approving purchases (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a business site in which you can embed the app (SAP Build Work Zone).

SAP CodeJam events part of the roadshow (Register Now!)

Below is a listing of the SAP CodeJam events on the roadshow schedule:

Dates

CodeJam topic

Location

Registration

8 August

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

9 August

SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code

São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

12 August

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

Santiago, Chile 🇨🇱

Link

13 August

SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code

Santiago, Chile 🇨🇱

Link

15 August

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

Buenos Aires, Argentina 🇦🇷

Link

16 August

SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code

Buenos Aires, Argentina 🇦🇷

Link

20 August

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

Bogotá, Colombia 🇨🇴

Link

21 August

SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code

Bogotá, Colombia 🇨🇴

Link

Make sure to register to secure your place. It is likely that the places available will go fast.

 

What if the CodeJam topics do not cover the SAP technology that you focus on? We may organise additional local events just to get together and have a chat.... similar to an SAP Stammstich. 

See you all somewhere in Latin America on the roadshow!

entry.title: ABAPConf Europe 2024 - 06.06.2024
entry.content:
ABAPConf Europe 2024 - 06.06.2024

 

Termin

SAP Stammtisch Wien

entry.title: İstanbul Community olarak ilk etkinliğimizi yaptık
entry.content:
Geçtiğimiz hafta, İstanbul Community olarak ilk etkinliğimizi gerçekleştirdik. İstanbul Kadıköy'de buluşarak, sektörün farklı iş kollarından gelen 15 grup üyemizle tanışma ve bilgi paylaşma fırsatı bulduk.

Her birimiz, geçmiş tecrübelerimizden yola çıkarak sektörde karşılaştığımız zorlukları ve başarıları paylaştık. Bu paylaşımlar, yeni bakış açıları kazanmamıza ve farklı çözüm yolları öğrenmemize olanak tanıdı.

Bu tür etkinliklere katılmak ve İstanbul Community'nin bir parçası olmak istiyorsanız, gruba üye olmayı unutmayın. Topluluğumuza katılarak, hem profesyonel ağınızı genişletebilir hem de sektörel gelişmelerden haberdar olabilirsiniz.

entry.title: SAP上海社区第一次线下聚会完满结束！
entry.content:
第一次我们选择在SAP Labs举办，由于没有Budget所以只准备了简单的零食，主题选择现在比较火热的AI话题，结合SAP的Strategy。活动中，各社区成员先自我介绍，然后由Alex Dong作为第一次话题的演讲者，讲述了SAP Business AI并现场演示了SAP如何利用GenAI为ERP数据做RAG搜索，最后大家各自相互认识，沟通交流。虽然第一次我们人数只有10人左右，但是汇集了SAP内部各个部门，SAP客户，SAP伙伴各色身份，比如来自MSG的20年+SAP经验的行业大拿，也有来自SAP的具有20年+ERP&BTP经验的技术专家，以及来自EY的伙伴大拿讲述SAP Integration Suite的实践历程。整个聚会，气氛轻松，内容接地气，是一次成功的线下实验！

未来，我们将继续举办类似线下聚会，并拓广到更多的合作伙伴和客户，争取引入更多更流行的话题！

entry.title: SAUG BTP Hackathon
entry.content:
During the build phase, the SAP team will assist and hand hold the teams to help them in the implementation of their SAP BTP use cases. SAUG and SAP will help you to work with SAP BTP and also help you build a working prototype for your business use cases. SAP’s team of global BTP experts will enable participating teams on the nuances of the implementation / usage of SAP BTP and will share SAP’s experience and knowledge. There will be 6 enablement webinars which will be deep dives of various BTP technologies and applications. 
 
 
Finalists will be invited to show off their projects in a Demo Jam at the SAUG National Summit in August. The winners will receive recognition and rewards and the whole community will benefit with outstanding learnings from real use cases.
 
We encourage members to send their submissions, nominate their teams and take part in the valuable knowledge building. This will be a great learning opportunity which will develop real-life BTP use cases and deliver direct business value to your organisation.

To know more details of the Hackathon and how to join, please refer to this webpage on SAUG Website

https://www.saug.com.au/Communities/SAUG_BTP_Hackathon

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch #5 Innovant : SAP Supers heroes
entry.content:
Le jeudi 18 avril dernier, la communauté SAP parisienne a vécu une soirée digne des Avengers : la 5ème édition du Stammtisch, désormais une institution incontournable ! Avec 78 super-participants, l'événement, sponsorisé par Malt, s'est déroulé dans leurs locaux et a explosé les records d'affluence. Ce fut un véritable moment de partage et d'échanges enrichissants. Et comme si ce n'était pas suffisant, trois super-développeurs advocates venus tout droit des États-Unis ont enfilé leurs capes pour partager leur expertise et dévoiler les dernières technologies SAP.

La soirée a commencé avec une présentation introduisant les membres de l’équipe des SAP Developers Advocate. Trois héros du code ont fait le déplacement pour partager leur mission :

  1. Thomas Jung : Manager de l’équipe
  2. Rich Heilman : Développeur Advocate pour les sujets ABAP, Steampunk, RAP
  3. Kevin Riedelsheimer : Développeur Advocate pour les sujets Cloud Native, CAP

Au cœur de la soirée, les participants ont découvert Joule, une solution révolutionnaire développée par SAP. Cette IA est un véritable super-pouvoir que les développeurs peuvent acquérir pour les aider à créer des applications SAP.

L'une des caractéristiques les plus remarquables de Joule réside dans sa connaissance approfondie de l'environnement de travail du développeur. Elle est capable de fournir des solutions adaptées et personnalisées durant le processus de développement. D’une simple phrase, Joule peut générer une application Fiori.

La soirée s'est conclue par une discussion ouverte, offrant à tous les participants l'opportunité d'échanger sur une variété de sujets. Cela a permis à chacun de partager ses idées, ses connaissances et ses expériences, contribuant ainsi à enrichir l'événement et à en faire un succès.

Un grand merci à notre sponsor, Malt, sans qui cette 5ème édition du Stammtisch n'aurait pas été possible. Grâce à eux, combiné à la présence des 3 mousquetaires @thomas_jung , @RichHeilman, @KRiedelsheimer, et aux participants, cette soirée a été un véritable succès et restera gravée dans nos mémoires comme un moment d'exception. Ensemble, nous avons prouvé une fois de plus que la communauté SAP est plus forte que jamais !

entry.title: Como receber notificações por e-mail da comunidade SAP e cadastro de Newsletters
entry.content:
Como receber notificações por e-mail da comunidade SAP e cadastro de Newsletters

(SAP Community Subscriptions with E-mail and Newsletters)

Em janeiro de 2024, a SAP atualizou toda a plataforma SAP Community, e os e-mails de novas publicações, dúvidas e artigos que você se inscreveu e recebeu através do e-mail SAP Central Communication Service notificação@sap.com (Exemplo na imagem abaixo: “New blog post"), não é mais enviado.

Neste blog, irei apresentar a nova forma de como você pode se inscrever para continuar recebendo e-mails da comunidade.

💡Vantagem: Ao se inscrever para acompanhar as publicações da comunidade, você fica atualizado sobre novas funcionalidades e artigos do seu interesse, além de poder interagir e responder dúvidas de outros membros da comunidade.

Ao efetuar login com seu P-User (Usuário pessoal) na plataforma SAP Community https://community.sap.com/ , na direita da tela, clique no ícone configurações > “My content”.

Depois, no seu perfil, procure a opção “My Settings”.

 

Em “Subscriptions and Notifications” você pode configurar como deseja ser notificado e o que deseja assinar.

Por exemplo, no final de uma publicação da comunidade, você encontrará “Tags gerenciadas pela SAP”, clique nas tags que deseja ser notificado por e-mail.

Apenas clique em: “Subscribe” nas tag do seu interesse.

Em “Subscriptions & Notifications” > “My Subscriptions”, você pode listar quais tags você está inscrito.

Além disto, em “Subscriptions & Notifications” > “Notification Settings”, é importante revisar suas Notificações por E-mail para garantir que você receberá o e-mail.

Na imagem abaixo, é um exemplo do novo formato de recebimento de e-mail com a notificações da comunidade SAP.

Newsletters SAP

Caso seu desejo seja se inscrever em uma Newsletters SAP, acesse o site: https://www.sap.com/dashboard/newsletters.html

Você pode assinar o boletim informativo principal da SAP, para atualizações semanais sobre estratégia SAP, notícias, atualizações e eventos para clientes, clientes potenciais e parceiros SAP.

Espero que este blog tenha ajudado você a Como receber notificações por e-mail da comunidade SAP e cadastro de Newsletters.

Se você achou este blog útil, curta, siga e compartilhe o conhecimento.

Fique à vontade para deixar seu comentário ou tirar qualquer dúvida relacionada ao tema.

Clovis Müller

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: May 22, 2024, Edition
entry.content:
No, your eyes do not deceive you. SAP Community Voice -- our long-time email newsletter -- is making the transition to What's New.

As announced in the May 22 edition of the newsletter, we're doing two more editions in June, then moving SAP Community Voice to What's New permanently.

To help everyone prepare for this change, we're starting to publish SAP Community Voice content to What's New now. In fact, the items here come straight from the May 22 edition of SAP Community Voice.

If you subscribe to the email newsletter, expect two more -- one on June 5 and June 19 -- before we make the permanent switch to What's New. After that, please make sure you've subscribed to What's New so you don't miss our biweekly breakdown of community highlights, news, and more!

For now, please enjoy these entries from our latest -- albeit a little late for here -- newsletter.

 

Featured Content

 

Participate in Global Running Day with Other SAP Community Members! 

We teased this event in the previous newsletter, and now we're happy to announce that one of our SAP Champions is driving a grassroots effort for Global Running Day. Runners will gather on June 5 to run the Tan in Melbourne, Australia, but you can participate virtually from June 1 to 9! 

Read the blog post

 

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q2 2024 Quarterly Release (QRC) 

The headline says it all: This blog post highlights the latest features in the Q2 2024 release of SAP Analytics Cloud. The post includes detailed information, as well as a video demonstrating what's new in this quarterly release. Our product experts cover everything! 

Read the blog post

 

First Steps for SAP Cloud ALM Deployment Scenario for ABAP Systems 

You'll need to set aside some time for this one, but once you read this thorough post, you'll be able to create a test landscape to practice with the SAP Cloud ALM deployment scenario without creating interferences with the real landscapes for the transport management system. 

Read the blog post

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Integration with SAP Datasphere

This post explains how to create a connection between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Datasphere, exploring integration strategies and real-world applications to achieve operational excellence, drive innovation and efficiency, and improve decision-making. 

Read the blog post

 

Community News

 

Getting the Most from SAP Community 

Per the introduction, What's New is the place to go for the latest announcements about SAP Community -- and it's also becoming the home for the SAP Community Voice newsletter. You'll want to subscribe here to stay up to date -- and also read our help section, watch our how-to videos, and take the Tour the SAP Community 2024 tutorial. 

 

Meet Your Peers Online and In Person at SAP Community Events! 

Global Running Day, a combination of in-person and virtual activities, isn't the only upcoming event scheduled by and for our members! Visit our events section to learn about SAP CodeJam, SAP Inside Track, and SAP Stammtisch meetups, as well as other online and in-person events.

 

Show Off Your Support for SAP Community Offline 

When you log out from SAP Community, do you still want the world to see how much your community membership means to you? Then you'll want to pick up something from our online store. We offer a variety of shirts, hoodies, socks, and more -- all proudly displaying the SAP Community name, so you can look stylish and smart at the same time.

entry.title: Re-imagining Innovation - Begin of SIT – ICOE AI hackathon – 2024
entry.content:
“Dream, Code, Achieve”

The recent AI Hackathon event was a remarkable success, marking a significant milestone in our ongoing efforts to foster technological innovation and collaboration. The event brought together talented students, industry experts, and academic leaders, creating a vibrant atmosphere of learning, creativity, and future-forward thinking.

Event & Certification Partners:

  • International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI, and Futuristic Technologies
  • SAP Insider Track Community Kolkata
  • Alien Brains Educations
  • Westernacher Consulting
  • Galelio AG 

Who participated?

All the engineering college students in West Bengal, All the Students from Colleges and Universities in Science and Commerce and relevant departments were eligible for the AI hackathon event. This hackathon was conducted in 2 Rounds.

Hackathon Timeline

11th April 2024: Round 1 (240 marks MCQ-based online exam)

12th April 2024: Round 1 Result Declaration (Top 25 teams advance to Round 2)

12th - 22nd April 2024: Round 2 (10-day Coding problem statement)

22nd April 2024: Final Submission Date of Round 2

25th April 2024: Announcement of Top 10 Teams

27th April 2024: SIT Community Event and Felicitation of Top 10 Teams

Core Team and Mentors

We are proud to have a distinguished panel of mentors and core team members guiding this event:

Prof. Amlan Chakrabarti: COO, International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI & Futuristic Technologies

Dr. Anindita Ganguly: Member, International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI & Futuristic Technologies

Dr. Saptarsi Goswami: Member, International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI & Futuristic Technologies

Sabyasachi Mukhopadhyay: Research Scholar (IIT KGP), Faculty, Google Developer Expert (ML)

Praveen Kumar Bahety: Founder, MD, Alien Brains, Sociana

Srini Gottimukkula: SIT VIP Panelist

Dipankar Saha: SAP Author & Mentor

 

Problem Statement

Theme:   Application of AI in Education

Topic 1:  AI for automated Essay Grading

  • Expectation is to find public datasets, with essays from students and grades by teachers.  
  • Build a model which takes the essay as an input assigns a score ( Free to use different open source models and fine tune)
  • More credit will be given which will explain the reason behind the score

Ref:

kaggle.com/competitions/asap-aes 

github.com/sankalpjain99/Automatic-Essay-Scoring 

frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/feduc.2020.572367/full 

 

Topic 2:  Bengali to English Translation for Science Subjects

  • Expectation is to find, build datasets with English to Bengali Translation
  • Build or leverage models (Free to explore openAI,  Bhasini etc.) for translation

Ref:

https://ieee-dataport.org/open-access/supara-benchmark-benchmark-dataset-english-bangla-machine-translation 

https://huggingface.co/saiful9379/Bangla-to-English-Translation 

 

Topic 3:  Student Performance Analysis

  • Expectation is to find public datasets with student performance
  • Correlate performance in different disciplines
  • Correlate student background with performance

Ref:

archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/320/student+performance 

https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/search/dataset/0038947/Learning-Poverty-Global-Database--Historical-data-and-sub-components 

Deliverable:

  • Working prototype in any environment like Google-colab
  • Presentation Explaining the same
  • Writeup should include future implementation plan with roadmap

A slot of 20 minutes will be given between 23rd to 25th April for the presentation of the same

30 marks on Demonstration 10 marks on presentation and 10 marks on Writeup

 

The Top 10

 

Sayandip Bhattacharyya

Generative Solutions

Crypto Coders

Cuber Savants

Zeus

Techie

Algorithm Architects

Web Homie

CamCoderr

Tech Strivers

Continued Support for Top Teams

In alignment with the advice of Dr. Anindita Ganguly, Alien Brains has decided to provide free guidance sessions to the Top 10 teams. This initiative aims to help these teams enhance their current projects significantly. Furthermore, once the ICOE campus is complete, these top-performing teams may receive an invitation for a special guidance session with ICOE professors, providing them with invaluable insights and mentorship.

Celebrating Success and Collaboration

Thanks to everyone involved for their opportunity and support throughout this journey. The event was a resounding success, with all participating students expressing their happiness and enthusiasm. We are pleased to announce that they have received their well-deserved certificates, symbolizing their hard work and dedication. It was a great experience for me to mentor and have experienced real time hackathon and looking forward to many more in Collab with @DataTherapist and other AI enthusiastic in the community.

entry.title: SAP Mentor Interview with Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Earlier this month I had the opportunity to interview Eric van Rossum, SAP’s Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP. You can watch the video of our discussion and read the highlights below, where you’ll also find related learning materials. Enjoy!

Eric van Rossum is the Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP at SAP, overseeing product marketing for the company's cloud ERP portfolio. He serves as the strategic bridge between product development and customer acquisition, ensuring product messaging and positioning are attuned to customer needs, and facilitating the successful launch and adoption of products in the market. Eric has been with SAP for 24 years and brings a wealth of experience across various functions including consulting, sales, corporate strategy, and support.

To help me summarize the content of the interview, I've used SAP Generative AI Hub :-). Check here what it ays are the highlights of our conversation: 

  • SAP has two cloud ERP offerings: RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP
  • RISE with SAP is targeted toward SAP’s installed-base customers, providing them a path to cloud ERP at their pace.
  • GROW with SAP is targeted toward net new customers to SAP, and installed base customers who want to take a greenfield approach to cloud ERP.
  • SAP is focusing on making ERP an enabler for business model changes, driving sustainability, and improving efficiency, as these are the key customer needs currently in the market.
  • With RISE and GROW, customers should be able to update their software regularly, making them innovation-ready and efficient, especially with the increasing prevalence of AI.
  • Eric believes the shift to the cloud also requires a paradigm shift within companies, citing an example of a customer who moved towards a standardized system and adopted SAP's out-of-the-box solutions.
  • One of the significant hurdles for customers adopting cloud ERP is the organizational readiness to standardize their processes, which requires a significant cultural shift.
  • Moving ERP to the cloud is seen as risky by some companies due to the critical role ERP systems play in company operations. They need strategies to transition without disrupting their business.
  • Eric is interested in a continued dialogue with the SAP Community, to hear from end users and improve SAP’s offerings to meet their needs better.

If you’re going to Sapphire in Orlando or Barcelona, you can hear more from Eric in these sessions:

SAP Sapphire Orlando (June 3-5)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP169)

  • Supercharge Your Business and Get Innovation Ready with RISE with SAP (Session code: ERP168)

  • The New ERP Mindset (Session code: TRE321)

SAP Sapphire Barcelona (June 11-13)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP146)

If you’re planning to join Sapphire virtual, you can hear more in these sessions:

  • ERP126 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Discover planned innovations

  • ERP127 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what is next

  • ERP200v | Accelerate business growth with S/4AHANA Cloud Public Edition

And to learn more about the topics discussed in this interview, the following learning journeys are especially recommended:

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP learning journey as a starting point to start learning about SAP Cloud ERP, how to distinguish SAP’s cloud ERP solutions and transformation offerings (GROW & RISE), establish a cloud mindset (fit-to-standard, SAP best practices, integration and extensibility, clean core), and get insights into business AI and sustainability fundamentals
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learning journey teaching fundamental implementation knowledge for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition leading to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation consultant certification complemented by quarterly live sessions, stay certified content and assessments focusing on AI capabilities and intelligent ERP innovations
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey teaching implementation fundamentals for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition leading to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation consultant certification, complemented by stay certified content and assessments focusing on how to Manage a Clean Core

Those fundamentals learning journeys are complemented by various line of business specific learning offerings and certifications focusing on business processes, best practices content, and configuration/integration/extensibility options

entry.title: Logistics Simplified: Resources, Insights, Workshops, and More for Smooth Adoption
entry.content:
Introducing you to the SAP Digital Logistics Adoption Campaigns!

Our mission is to empower you with the knowledge and tools needed to fully leverage the capabilities of Logistics solutions. This program is designed to help you understand the unique value these solutions bring to your business, guiding you through every step of the adoption journey. You’ll find comprehensive resources, expert insights, customer experience sharing, and practical tips to accelerate implementation, ensuring a smooth Go-Live, and maximizing the benefits for your business!

Our current campaign focus is on S/4HANA Transportation Management and S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management. Join us on this journey through just a few simple steps!

Step 1: Access the Solution Value Card

A value card is a one-pager with key information on the solution, such as its value proposition, key capabilities, and quick recourses. It also indicates what to expect from the Adoption campaign. 

  • S/4HANA Transportation Management
  • S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

Step 2: Participate in Expert Guided Adoption Series

To further support your journey, we have organized workshops that are crafted to provide in-depth training, featuring interactive sessions, real-world scenarios, and expert-led discussions. Whether you're just getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, these sessions will equip you with actionable strategies and best practices to drive successful adoption and consumption. Join us to enhance your skills, connect with peers, and challenge us with your unique questions so we can understand your needs better!

S/4HANA Transportation Management

  • Getting Started with S/4HANA Transportation Management: PDF | Recording
  • June 25, 2024: Baseline Activation and Setups
  • July 18, 2024: Optimizer & Package Building
  • August 20, 2024: Insights & Analytics

S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

  • Overview & Value Realization: PDF | Recording
  • Feature Implementation Best Practices: PDF | Recording
  • June 13, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Consumer Products
  • June 27, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Automotive
  • July 11, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Retail
  • August 8, 2024: Business AI Adoption in Logistics

We hope these sessions enhance your understanding and ability to leverage the solution, and we hope to see you in one of our upcoming workshops! If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to email us.
